hints.text: **Very hilly & forested**; much coverage on small roads **deep inside forest**
Alpine-inspired: light walls, dark wood, **half-hipped roofs**; **kozolec** hay sheds (thin wooden beams w/ gaps) = very typical
hints.uniq_note: The kozolec hay rack is THE Slovenian countryside tell.

hints.text: **Slovene: only Č Š Ž** (Croatian adds Ć and Đ)

shared_with.diff: Ć or Đ on a sign → Croatia; ije → Croatian too.

hints.text: Bollards white w/ black top, **bright-red front / white back reflector**

shared_with.note: Same shape — **dark**-red front + black/grey back there.

hints.text: Directional signs **yellow w/ black border** (ex-Yugoslav standard; Austria: white/blue); km markers = unique layout (yellow road-number box top-left, black divider, distance below; Austria: blue)
hints.uniq_note: The km-marker layout is Slovenia-specific; the yellow directional signs are pan-ex-Yugoslav.

hints.text: Chevrons red-on-white (rarer black-on-white); ped signs 5 stripes + **beltless person** (Croatia: belt; Austria: dotted lines)
**Long outer dashes** on roads (neighbours rarely dash outer lines)
Street signs white w/ metal border on posts, or **red on house walls** ('ulica' in the name; Czech red signs never say ulica); house numbers **red w/ white text**

shared_with.note: Red wall signs + red house numbers there too.

hints.text: **A-type guardrails** (rounded edges) — the CR-50-50 decider: Croatia uses B-type (square edges)

Tallest mountains → **NW Alpine region**; east = flat/rolling fields w/o mountains in view; coast = white cliffs + bushes + vineyards + Mediterranean houses; SE = wide E-W valley between ranges; **Italian bilingual signs** → far west
**Ljubljana** = GREEN house numbers (+occasionally green street signs); Celje = signs w/ blue shield + 3 yellow stars; Trbovlje = blue-green shield w/ black crest
